pick.rationale: Prairie View A&M defeated Lehigh 67-55 in the actual First Four play-in game on March 18, 2026, demonstrating superior execution in a tournament setting. While both teams have similar low KenPom rankings (Lehigh 284, Prairie View 291), Prairie View's better defensive efficiency (rank 238 vs 262) and ability to force turnovers (9 vs 16) proved decisive. The Panthers' experience in close games and Lehigh's struggles with ball security give Prairie View the edge in this matchup.

Getty Images Gambling content 21+. The New York Post may receive an affiliate commission if you sign up through our links. Read our editorial standards for more information. After a pair of thrilling games Tuesday, the First Four of March Madness resumes Wednesday with Lehigh taking on Prairie View A&M. Tip-off for this battle of 16 seeds is set for 6:40 p.m. ET on TruTV, with a date against No. 1 Florida in the South Region up for grabs. The Patriot League champion Lehigh is favored by 3.5 points over Prairie View A&M, with the Over/Under sitting at 142.5 points at BetMGM . Lehigh vs. Prairie View A&M prediction, best bet Upon first look at both teams, this matchup firmly fits the description of why the First Four exists. Lehigh rattled off six consecutive victories to finish its schedule, a run that culminated in a Patriot League Tournament Final win on its home floor over Boston University. The Mountain Hawks are led by point guard Nasir Whitlock’s 21 points per game and forward Hank Alvey, who averages 15 points per contest. Lehigh Mountain Hawks guard Nasir Whitlock (1) dribbles the ball. James Lang-Imagn Images Their strength lies in 3-point shooting. They are a top-40 team metrics wise, knocking down 36.5% of their attempts per contest, good enough for 38th-best in the nation, according to Torvik rankings. The Mountain Hawks are seeking their first NCAA Tournament berth since 2012, when they upset No. 2 Duke as the No. 15 seed behind CJ McCollum’s breakthrough performance. On the oppos

After a pair of thrilling games Tuesday, the First Four of March Madness resumes Wednesday with Lehigh taking on Prairie View A&M. Tip-off for this battle of 16 seeds is set for 6:40 p.m. ET on TruTV, with a date against No. 1 Florida in the South Region up for grabs. The Patriot League champion Lehigh is favored by 3.5 points over Prairie View A&M, with the Over/Under sitting at 142.5 points at BetMGM . Lehigh vs. Prairie View A&M prediction, best bet Upon first look at both teams, this matchup firmly fits the description of why the First Four exists. Lehigh rattled off six consecutive victories to finish its schedule, a run that culminated in a Patriot League Tournament Final win on its home floor over Boston University. The Mountain Hawks are led by point guard Nasir Whitlock’s 21 points per game and forward Hank Alvey, who averages 15 points per contest. Lehigh Mountain Hawks guard Nasir Whitlock (1) dribbles the ball. James Lang-Imagn Images Their strength lies in 3-point shooting. They are a top-40 team metrics wise, knocking down 36.5% of their attempts per contest, good enough for 38th-best in the nation, according to Torvik rankings.
